whatItMeasures: Fasting glucose measures the concentration of glucose (sugar) in the blood after at least 8 hours without eating; it is used to screen for, diagnose, and monitor diabetes and prediabetes.
whatItMeasuresSource: American Diabetes Association. Standards of Care in Diabetes. https://diabetesjournals.org/care/article/46/Supplement_1/S1/148054

referenceRanges.source: American Diabetes Association. Diagnosis and Classification of Diabetes. 2024.
American Diabetes Association. Diagnosis and Classification of Diabetes. 2024.
American Diabetes Association. Diagnosis and Classification of Diabetes. 2024.

causesOfHigh: Cushing's syndrome (excess cortisol raises blood sugar)
Pancreatitis or pancreatic disease (reduced insulin production)
Stress or acute illness (counterregulatory hormones raise glucose)
causesOfLow: Excess insulin (insulin medication overdose or insulinoma)
Intense or prolonged exercise without adequate carbohydrate intake
Alcohol consumption (especially without food — alcohol blocks glucose release from the liver)
Reactive hypoglycemia (blood sugar drops 2–4 hours after eating)

high: Numbness or tingling in hands and feet (in chronic hyperglycaemia)
low: Seizures or loss of consciousness (in severe hypoglycaemia)

symptomsSource: NHS. Type 2 diabetes symptoms. https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/type-2-diabetes/symptoms/; NHS. Hypoglycaemia symptoms. https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/low-blood-sugar-hypoglycaemia/symptoms/
